profile.name: Karl E. Nell
roles: Director of Technology Strategy, Raytheon Technologies
organizations: United States Army - Intelligence and Security Command (INSCOM)
profile.summary: Karl Nell is a retired U.S. Army Colonel who served in Army Intelligence and Security Command (INSCOM) and Special Forces before transitioning to defense industry and UAP advocacy. He co-founded the Sol Foundation - a Stanford-affiliated academic organization focused on rigorous UAP research - alongside David Grusch and Dr. Garry Nolan. On July 26, 2023, Nell testified before the House Oversight Committee alongside Grusch, Ryan Graves, and David Fravor, providing senior military corroboration for the UAP disclosure narrative from a career intelligence and special operations perspective. His most cited statements: 'Non-human intelligence exists and has been interacting with humanity' and that UAP transitions to public acknowledgment are 'imminent' and 'inevitable.'
education: B.S. - U.S. Military Academy, West Point (1988)
M.S. Aeronautics and Astronautics - Massachusetts Institute of Technology
early_career: U.S. Military Academy West Point graduate 1988
Multiple intelligence and special operations assignments across career
INSCOM - Army Intelligence and Security Command senior roles

key_events.event: Graduates West Point; begins Army career
Career in Army Special Forces and INSCOM; multiple classified intelligence roles spanning 28 years
Senior Army role involving UAP-adjacent programs (specifics classified); acquires knowledge base that informs later public testimony
Retires from Army; joins Raytheon Technologies as Director of Technology Strategy
Begins active engagement with UAP research community; connects with Grusch and Garry Nolan

Testifies before House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security alongside Grusch, Graves, and Fravor; delivers statement corroborating existence of non-human intelligence and classified UAP programs
Co-founds Sol Foundation alongside David Grusch, Dr. Garry Nolan, and Dr. Peter Skafish; organization holds inaugural symposium at Stanford University
Sol Foundation inaugural symposium at Stanford brings together academics, scientists, and former government officials
Continues public advocacy through Sol Foundation; participates in multiple UAP conferences and media appearances

testimony.hearing: House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security, Border, and Foreign Affairs
key_statements: Non-human intelligence exists and has been interacting with humanity - this is not a fringe position but one held by serious intelligence professionals.
The UAP transition to public acknowledgment is not a matter of if, but when. It is imminent.
I have seen evidence. I cannot discuss specifics due to ongoing classification, but I can tell you that what Grusch is describing is consistent with what I know.
The reason these programs are concealed is not malice - it is compartmentalization that has taken on a life of its own.
testimony.context: Nell provided senior military corroboration without making specific program disclosures, lending institutional credibility to Grusch's more specific claims. His testimony was notable for its measured, professional tone and the weight carried by his West Point / INSCOM background.
testimony.classification_constraints: Nell stated explicitly that he could not discuss program specifics due to ongoing classification. His general corroboration is significant precisely because it comes within those constraints.

sol_foundation.mission: Rigorous, academically grounded research into UAP - bridging the gap between government insiders, scientific institutions, and public discourse.
sol_foundation.inaugural_symposium: November 2023 at Stanford University; brought together academics, scientists, and former government officials for the first major academic conference on UAP
sol_foundation.significance: The Sol Foundation represents the first serious attempt to anchor UAP research in credentialed academic institutions rather than independent advocacy or entertainment media. Nell's role as treasurer signals organizational and financial seriousness beyond the typical UAP advocacy model.

claims.claim: Non-human intelligence exists and has been interacting with humanity. This is a conclusion held by serious intelligence professionals, not a fringe position.
claims.notes: Stated under oath in July 2023 congressional testimony. Corroborates Grusch without providing specific program evidence. No independent verification of the broader claim exists publicly.

claims.claim: The UAP transition to public acknowledgment is imminent and inevitable - a matter of when, not if.
claims.notes: Personal assessment rather than specific program claim. Consistent with broader disclosure narrative. The March 2024 AARO report represents the opposite institutional position.

claims.claim: Grusch's specific disclosures are consistent with information Nell has independently encountered through his career in Army intelligence.
claims.notes: Stated under oath. Corroborating testimony from a West Point / INSCOM officer adds credibility but does not constitute independent public evidence. Nell has not disclosed specifics.

claims.claim: The concealment of UAP programs is the result of extreme compartmentalization that has exceeded its original oversight structure - not simply deliberate malice or a single decision.
claims.notes: Consistent with how modern SAP structures actually work. Aligns with testimony from Grusch and Elizondo about bureaucratic access denial.

claims.claim: Elements of the U.S. government possess knowledge of UAP programs that has not been made available to Congress, AARO, or the public through proper oversight channels.
claims.notes: The ICIG deemed Grusch's related complaint 'credible and urgent' - the closest to institutional verification of this general claim. AARO formally denies it.

supporting: West Point graduate, 28-year Army career including INSCOM and Special Forces - high institutional credibility
Testified under oath before Congress in July 2023
Co-founded Sol Foundation with David Grusch and Dr. Garry Nolan (Stanford) - signals serious academic commitment
Consistent professional tone; careful distinction between what can and cannot be disclosed due to classification
MIT graduate degree in aeronautics - technical sophistication
contradicting: Has not made specific program disclosures - corroborating testimony without specific evidence is difficult to independently evaluate

associated_people.relationship: Testified alongside Nell at the July 26, 2023 House Oversight hearing; Fravor's direct encounter provides the experiential anchor for the broader disclosure narrative Nell supports

associated_people.name: Dr. Garry Nolan
associated_people.role: Professor of Pathology, Stanford University; Sol Foundation co-founder
associated_people.relationship: Co-founded Sol Foundation with Nell; Nolan's Stanford affiliation and scientific credibility provide academic legitimacy to the organization Nell helped establish

associated_people.name: Luis Elizondo
associated_people.role: Former Director, AATIP; co-founder, To The Stars Academy
associated_people.relationship: Contemporaneous figure in the UAP disclosure movement; Elizondo's institutional claims about compartmentalized programs are consistent with Nell's general corroboration

disclosures.title: House Oversight Subcommittee on National Security - UAP Hearing

disclosures.notes: Ongoing public advocacy through Sol Foundation events and UAP conferences; continues to maintain careful distinction between classifiable and public information
